response: {"purpose":"Figure 2a shows the distribution of fold-enrichment values for all 23,034 CLIPper-identified SLBP eCLIP clusters relative to their paired Size-Matched Input (SMInput), using a histogram that distinguishes histone-overlapping clusters (pink) from the broader cluster population. The panel establishes the baseline rationale for SMInput normalization by demonstrating that histone-overlapping clusters — the expected high-confidence binding sites for SLBP — are preferentially concentrated at higher fold-enrichment values when signal is compared against the paired input control. This matters because it provides direct visual evidence that SMInput normalization improves the ability to distinguish true SLBP binding events from background noise, motivating the normalization strategy evaluated in subsequent panels of the figure.","title":"SMInput Normalization Enriches SLBP Histone Cluster Signal Over Background"}
